seoTitle: Main Beach vs Malanda — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Main Beach vs Malanda: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Main Beach and Malanda.
heading: Main Beach vs Malanda — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Main Beach, Queensland and Malanda, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Main Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Malanda (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Main Beach or Malanda safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Main Beach has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Malanda scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Main Beach scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Malanda scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Main Beach or Malanda?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Main Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
